akuma-stand-light-punch.name: Standing Light Punch
akuma-stand-light-punch.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; fastest chain timing (more useful vs. DI)

akuma-stand-medium-punch.name: Standing Medium Punch
akuma-stand-medium-punch.notes_sc: 2f extra recovery on whiff; primary tool for block pressure (high active frame count makes it strong for oki); leads into much stronger combos on counter-hit

akuma-stand-heavy-punch.name: Standing Heavy Punch
akuma-stand-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Long-range hitconfirmable poke with a great hitbox; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame

akuma-stand-light-kick.name: Standing Light Kick
akuma-stand-light-kick.notes_sc: Good range for a light normal; useful in light confirms, punishes, and neutral buffers; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

akuma-stand-medium-kick.name: Standing Medium Kick
akuma-stand-medium-kick.notes_sc: Forces stand (important for 214LK in combos and whiff punishes); high active frame count and good hitbox for counterpoking and for meaty pressure; only 15f to hitconfirm TC cancel without CH/PC/DR

akuma-stand-heavy-kick.name: Standing Heavy Kick
notes: Forces the opponent into a standing position
1 frames of recovery added when the attack misses
akuma-stand-heavy-kick.notes_sc: 2 hits; 1st hit forces stand (important for combos); both the 2nd hit and the farthest-reaching hitbox of 1st hit whiff on crouching opponents unless they extend a vertical hurtbox; if 1st hit is stand blocked, both hits will connect on block; 1f extra recovery when 2nd hit whiffs; Counter-hit/Punish Counter advantage carries through both hits; great punish starter since scaling doesn't apply; both hits put airborne opponents into limited juggle state

akuma-crouch-light-punch.name: Crouching Light Punch
akuma-crouch-light-punch.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; Akuma's best light normal for combos due to 5LK link and 5MK counter-hit link; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

akuma-crouch-medium-punch.name: Crouching Medium Punch
akuma-crouch-medium-punch.notes_sc: 2f extra recovery on whiff; excellent button for combos, midscreen buffers, and Drive Rush pressure (more rewarding than 5MP); topmost hurtbox on frames 1-8 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air); extends a wider hurtbox until the end of recovery

akuma-crouch-heavy-punch.name: Crouching Heavy Punch
akuma-crouch-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Forces stand (important combo/punish tool for 214LK); head/arm hurtbox is anti-air invuln while active on frames 8-15; good anti-air for vertical approaches but cannot hit cross-up (anti-air hitbox takes ~11f to come out, making it slower than average); only cancelable on active frames 1-3 with 1f extra hitstun on these frames

akuma-crouch-medium-kick.name: Crouching Medium Kick
akuma-crouch-medium-kick.notes_sc: Extends a wide hurtbox 3f before startup and during recovery; special/DR cancel is delayed until after active frames; DI cancel can occur on 1st active frame

akuma-crouch-heavy-kick.name: Crouching Heavy Kick
notes: Knockdown time extended when attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter

akuma-jump-light-punch.name: Jumping Light Punch
akuma-jump-light-punch.notes_sc: Fast air-to-air; can be used as a fuzzy instant overhead

akuma-jump-medium-punch.name: Jumping Medium Punch
notes: Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent
akuma-jump-medium-punch.notes_sc: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state but no juggle potential on its own; lenient cancel window to juggle into j.214K/j.236P/Air SA1; lower body projectile invuln 8-23f (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

akuma-jump-heavy-punch.name: Jumping Heavy Punch
notes: Forces a slam knockdown on counter or Punish Counter when hitting a mid-air opponent
akuma-jump-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents on Counter-hit/Punish Counter; good hitbox priority for air approaches; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

akuma-jump-light-kick.name: Jumping Light Kick
notes: Has cross-up attack properties

akuma-jump-medium-kick.name: Jumping Medium Kick
notes: Has cross-up attack properties
akuma-jump-medium-kick.notes_sc: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

akuma-jump-heavy-kick.name: Jumping Heavy Kick
akuma-jump-heavy-kick.notes_sc: Most horizontal range of Akuma's air normals; lower body projectile invuln 12-19f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

skull-splitter.name: Skull Splitter
notes: An overhead attack that cannot be blocked while crouching.
Second attack becomes an overhead only if the first attack whiffs
skull-splitter.notes_sc: 2-hits; fast overhead with excellent reward (5MK) when used out of Drive Rush; if blocked, Akuma is out of throw range (can frame trap with CH 2LP into 5MK to punish reversal 4f jabs); 2nd hit is no longer an overhead if the first hit connects

resso-snap-kick.name: Resso Snap Kick
notes: A quick, long-reaching snap kick.
resso-snap-kick.notes_sc: 3f extra recovery on block, 4f extra recovery on whiff; whiffed version extends a wider hurtbox during recovery; long-range poke with great hitbox priority; good whiff punish tool that allows a link to 2HK from closer ranges, or into SA2 from around Akuma's 2MK range; safe from all reversal 4f normal even at point blank (except Zangief 2LK, which gets no follow-up)

rago-high-kick.name: Rago High Kick
notes: A launching kick that can be canceled into special moves.
rago-high-kick.notes_sc: Puts opponents into limited juggle state (free juggle if used out of Drive Rush); upper body and kicking leg is anti-air invuln while active (frames 12-16); unreliable anti-air due to slow startup and extended vertical hurtbox before the invuln starts; being a cancelable launcher allows Akuma to juggle into Demon Raid follow-ups or charged fireballs

viscera-piercer.name: Viscera Piercer
notes: A quick series of attacks that can be canceled by a special move to create combos, or eliminate gaps that would leave you open to counterattack.
viscera-piercer.notes_sc: Important combo/hitconfirm tool; always a true blockstring from 5MP

bone-crusher-axe-kick.name: Bone Crusher Axe Kick
notes: A two-hit series of attacks, the second of which cannot be blocked crouching.
bone-crusher-axe-kick.notes_sc: Safe overhead Target Combo that's great for chipping Drive gauge; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle OTG state (can be used in juggles where 5MK connects, like wallsplat or DR~5MK routes); interruptible 5f gap between hits on block (up to 8f at max delay); will not combo on final 2 cancelable frames of 5MK without CH/PC/DR starter making it very difficult to hitconfirm

kikoku-combination-1.name: Kikoku Combination (1)
notes: A multi-hit attack with a lenient input window for follow-ups.

kikoku-combination-2.name: Kikoku Combination (2)
notes: A multi-hit attack with a lenient input window for follow-ups.

kikoku-combination-3.name: Kikoku Combination (3)
notes: A multi-hit attack with a lenient input window for follow-ups.

tenmaku-blade-kick.name: Tenmaku Blade Kick
notes: A downward attack that doesn't leave you open, and allows you to change the trajectory of your forward jump.
During a forward jump
tenmaku-blade-kick.notes_sc: Forward Jump only; 6f extra landing recovery on whiff; great tool for mixing up jump timings, especially against characters that rely on non-invincible anti-airs; much stronger hurtbox compared to other divekicks; cannot hit cross-up; all landing recovery after the first 2 frames has a crouch-sized hurtbox, and the final recovery frame is in a crouching state; kicking leg is projectile invuln on frame 14 until landing

gou-hadoken-light.name: Gou Hadoken (Light)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range.
Hold and release the button for 25 frames or more to activate Level 2
Hold and release the button for 49 frames or more to activate Level 3

gou-hadoken-light-charge-level-2.name: Gou Hadoken (Light) (Charge Level 2)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range.

gou-hadoken-light-charge-level-3.name: Gou Hadoken (Light) (Charge Level 3)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range.

gou-hadoken-medium.name: Gou Hadoken (Medium)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range.
Hold and release the button for 25 frames or more to activate Level 2
Hold and release the button for 49 frames or more to activate Level 3

gou-hadoken-medium-charge-level-2.name: Gou Hadoken (Medium) (Charge Level 2)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range.

gou-hadoken-medium-charge-level-3.name: Gou Hadoken (Medium) (Charge Level 3)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range.

gou-hadoken-heavy.name: Gou Hadoken (Heavy)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range.
Charged
Hold and release the button for 25 frames or more to activate Level 2
Hold and release the button for 49 frames or more to activate Level 3

gou-hadoken-heavy-charge-level-2.name: Gou Hadoken (Heavy) (Charge Level 2)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range.

gou-hadoken-heavy-charge-level-3.name: Gou Hadoken (Heavy) (Charge Level 3)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range.

gou-hadoken-od.name: Gou Hadoken (OD)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range.

gou-hadoken-charged.name: Gou Hadoken (Charged) (OD)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range.
Hold down the button for over 25 frames to change its properties

zanku-hadoken.name: Zanku Hadoken
notes: Fire a ki blast from the air to the ground below. This attack is effective as a tool to shield your approach. The Overdrive version can also be performed from a neutral jump.
During a forward jump
Angle of the projectile changes depending on the button pressed
zanku-hadoken.notes_sc: Forward/Neutral Jump only on airborne frame 9-33; 3-hit OD projectile; dissipates if Akuma is hit; creates stationary projectile on frames 6-20, then shoots downward at ~50 degree angle (this projectile will only have 2 hits if the initial hitbox collided with opponent); on hit only, landing recovery after 2f is cancelable into any action except walking/jumping; projectile hitbox is considered an airborne attack (can be avoided with anti-air invuln moves); puts airborne opponents into free juggle state (or maintains existing juggle state); puts grounded opponents into limited juggle state; advantage varies significantly depending on timing/spacing/angle (at worst, approximately -3 oB vs. tall characters, -1 oB vs. standard characters, but +1~3 if all hits Perfect Parried); foot hurtbox is projectile invuln 1f~land; upper half of hitbox only connects on opponents mid-combo

zanku-hadoken-od.name: Zanku Hadoken (OD)
notes: Fire a ki blast from the air to the ground below. This attack is effective as a tool to shield your approach. The Overdrive version can also be performed from a neutral jump.

gou-shoryuken-light.name: Gou Shoryuken (Light)
notes: Leap into the air and perform a powerful uppercut. A Gou Shoryuken is invincible to an opponent's jumping attacks, making it a great anti-air tool.
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 14
Considered airborne from frames 8 - 35

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 14

gou-shoryuken-medium.name: Gou Shoryuken (Medium)
notes: Leap into the air and perform a powerful uppercut. A Gou Shoryuken is invincible to an opponent's jumping attacks, making it a great anti-air tool.
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 9
Considered airborne from frames 9 - 45

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 9

gou-shoryuken-heavy.name: Gou Shoryuken (Heavy)
notes: Leap into the air and perform a powerful uppercut. A Gou Shoryuken is invincible to an opponent's jumping attacks, making it a great anti-air tool.
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 9
Considered airborne from frames 7 - 52

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 9

gou-shoryuken-od.name: Gou Shoryuken (OD)
notes: Leap into the air and perform a powerful uppercut. A Gou Shoryuken is invincible to an opponent's jumping attacks, making it a great anti-air tool.
Completely invincible from frames 1 - 8.
Considered airborne from frames 8 - 53.

tatsumaki-zankukyaku-light.name: Tatsumaki Zankukyaku (Light)
notes: Unleash a series of roundhouse kicks while moving forward. Useful in combos as well as for sending opponents flying away.
6 frames of recovery added when the attack misses

tatsumaki-zankukyaku-medium.name: Tatsumaki Zankukyaku (Medium)
notes: Unleash a series of roundhouse kicks while moving forward. Useful in combos as well as for sending opponents flying away.

tatsumaki-zankukyaku-heavy.name: Tatsumaki Zankukyaku (Heavy)
notes: Unleash a series of roundhouse kicks while moving forward. Useful in combos as well as for sending opponents flying away.

tatsumaki-zankukyaku-od.name: Tatsumaki Zankukyaku (OD)
notes: Unleash a series of roundhouse kicks while moving forward. Useful in combos as well as for sending opponents flying away.

aerial-tatsumaki-zankukyaku.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Zankukyaku
notes: A Tatsumaki Zanku-kyaku performed during a jump. Useful to change jump trajectory or to finish up combos.
During a forward jump
During a forward jump

aerial-tatsumaki-zankukyaku-od.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Zankukyaku (OD)
notes: A Tatsumaki Zanku-kyaku performed during a jump. Useful to change jump trajectory or to finish up combos.

adamant-flame-light-1.name: Adamant Flame (Light 1)
notes: A quick thrust engulfed in flames. Useful for exploiting an opponent's opening, or in combos.
Can transition to branching attacks from frames 19 - 22

adamant-flame-light-2.name: Adamant Flame (Light 2)
notes: A quick thrust engulfed in flames. Useful for exploiting an opponent's opening, or in combos.

adamant-flame-medium-1.name: Adamant Flame (Medium 1)
notes: A quick thrust engulfed in flames. Useful for exploiting an opponent's opening, or in combos.
Can transition to branching attacks from frames 23 - 26
3 frames of recovery added when the attack misses
Adds +10 frames of advantage on a Punish Counter

adamant-flame-medium-2.name: Adamant Flame (Medium 2)
notes: A quick thrust engulfed in flames. Useful for exploiting an opponent's opening, or in combos.

adamant-flame-heavy-1.name: Adamant Flame (Heavy 1)
notes: A quick thrust engulfed in flames. Useful for exploiting an opponent's opening, or in combos.
Can transition to branching attacks from frames 27 - 30
2 frames of recovery added when the attack misses
Adds +12 frames of advantage on a Punish Counter

adamant-flame-heavy-2.name: Adamant Flame (Heavy 2)
notes: A quick thrust engulfed in flames. Useful for exploiting an opponent's opening, or in combos.

adamant-flame-od-1.name: Adamant Flame (OD 1)
notes: A quick thrust engulfed in flames. Useful for exploiting an opponent's opening, or in combos.
Can transition to branching attacks from frames 22 - 25
Produces a crumple stun for 39 frames when the attack lands as a Punish Counter

adamant-flame-od-2.name: Adamant Flame (OD 2)
notes: A quick thrust engulfed in flames. Useful for exploiting an opponent's opening, or in combos.

demon-raid.name: Demon Raid
notes: A quick leap towards the opponent that allows you to transition into various techniques. Clever use of these techniques will help strengthen your offense.
Can go into any transition attack except for Demon Low Slash and Demon Swoop between frames 18 - 40
Considered airborne after frame 7
Distance traveled is determined by the button pressed
demon-raid.notes_sc: Tracks opponent's location regardless of distance; counter-hit state for entire duration; cancelable into follow-ups on frames 16-40 (16-38 for Fireball/Tatsu and Air SA1 follow-ups); on frame 41, automatically transitions to Demon Low Slash (or Demon Swoop if holding Down)

demon-raid-od.name: Demon Raid (OD)
notes: A quick leap towards the opponent that allows you to transition into various techniques. Clever use of these techniques will help strengthen your offense.
Can go into any transition attack except for Demon Low Slash and Demon Swoop between frames 16 - 40
Considered airborne after frame 7

demon-low-slash.name: Demon Low Slash
notes: Performs a low sweep the moment Demon Raid lands back on the ground. The kick has a long reach, and allows you to act before an opponent even if blocked.
During overdrive demon raid
No input
demon-low-slash.notes_sc: 4f extra recovery on whiff; recovery is in a crouching state until the final frame (final 5f on whiff); causes Akuma to auto-correct if Demon Raid crosses up; can be spaced for better frame advantage

demon-guillotine.name: Demon Guillotine
notes: A vicious downward chop performed from Demon Raid. This is an overhead attack, and allows you to act before an opponent even if blocked.
During overdrive demon raid
Recovery changes depending strength of the attack blocked
demon-guillotine.notes_sc: Overhead with many active frames, making it good for meaty pressure; strong hitbox and mid-air pause makes it more difficult to anti-air; cannot hit cross-up; frames 4-8 of landing recovery have a crouch-sized hurtbox (but still standing)

demon-blade-kick.name: Demon Blade Kick
notes: A quick downward kick performed from Demon Raid. The button used determines your angle of descent.
During overdrive demon raid
Recovery changes depending on the height of the attack when it hits or is blocked
5 frames of recovery added when the attack misses
demon-blade-kick.notes_sc: 5f extra recovery on whiff; button strength determines angle (LK short, MK mid, HK far); puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state (or maintains juggle state if used mid-combo); extends a large projectile invincible hurtbox on frame 11 until landing (making the divekick easier to anti-air while still being useful for avoiding fireballs)

demon-swoop.name: Demon Swoop
notes: A sudden drop to the ground performed from Demon Raid that eliminates any openings. Mix this in with other Demon Raid finishers to keep your opponent guessing.
During overdrive demon raid
demon-swoop.notes_sc: Acts like an empty jump (with the same 45f duration) with adjustable distance; 5f extra landing recovery when crossing over opponent; useful for baiting opponent's anti-air, Drive Impact, or Parry; must be inputting any Down direction on frame 41 of 236K; cannot block during landing recovery, and Akuma is in a counter-hit state for entire duration; crouching state on final recovery frame

demon-gou-zanku.name: Demon Gou Zanku
notes: An attack only available from Overdrive Demon Raid. Useful in combos or for a surprise attack, it doesn't consume any additional Drive.
During overdrive od demon raid
demon-gou-zanku.notes_sc: 3-hit OD projectile; can be input on frame 16-38 of 236KK (unless Gou Hadoken is on-screen); does not consume additional Drive gauge; dissipates if Akuma is hit; creates stationary projectile on frames 6-20, then shoots downward at ~50 degree angle (this projectile will only have 2 hits if the initial hitbox collided with opponent); on hit only, landing recovery after 2f is cancelable into any action except walking/jumping; projectile hitbox is considered an airborne attack (can be avoided with anti-air invuln moves); puts airborne opponents into free juggle state (or maintains existing juggle state); puts grounded opponents into limited juggle state; advantage varies significantly depending on timing/spacing/angle; foot hurtbox is projectile invuln 1f~land

demon-gou-rasen.name: Demon Gou Rasen
notes: An attack only available from Overdrive Demon Raid. Useful in combos against an airborne opponent, it doesn't consume any additional Drive.
During overdrive od demon raid
demon-gou-rasen.notes_sc: Can be input on frame 16-38 of 236KK (unless Gou Hadoken is on-screen); does not consume additional Drive gauge; cannot hit cross-up or against grounded opponents; stops Akuma's air momentum; performs final kick on hit only, which slams opponent down into a limited juggle OTG state; KD advantage depends on how high it connects (useful after 4HK launch, allowing most juggle enders to combo); Akuma is in a crouching state on frames 4-10 of landing recovery; lower foot/leg hurtbox is projectile invuln 1f~land

ashura-senku-forward.name: Ashura Senku (Forward)
notes: A technique that grants the user swift movement while cloaking them in illusions. Input forward or back together with the other buttons to move in that direction.
ashura-senku-forward.notes_sc: Cannot be canceled into; no collision box on frames 17-35 (can pass through opponent); no invincibility whatsoever, but passing through opponent may cause attack hitbox to whiff; counter-hit state for entire duration; frames 24-38 are cancelable into Oboro Throw follow-up; Distance: 3.663

ashura-senku-backward.name: Ashura Senku (Backward)
notes: A technique that grants the user swift movement while cloaking them in illusions. Input forward or back together with the other buttons to move in that direction.
ashura-senku-backward.notes_sc: Cannot be canceled into; travels back ~1/2 screen; no collision box on frames 15-37; no invincibility whatsoever; counter-hit state for entire duration; Distance: 2.128

oboro-throw.name: Oboro Throw
notes: A throw available during Ashura Senku. Useful at mid-range against opponents with a tendency to block.
During ashura senku (forward)
Properties change to the following when the attack lands as a Punish Counter:
- Deals 2900 damage
- Super Art gauge increases by 4000
oboro-throw.notes_sc: Total startup 31-45f depending on input timing; can be activated even after passing through opponent; 1.076 Range does not including 6KKK travel distance (maximum total range of 3.697)

messatsu-gohado.name: Messatsu Gohado
notes: Fires a high-speed, concentrated ball of the Satsui no Hado forward. Has high attack power for a projectile-based Super Art.
Invincible to strikes and projectiles from frames 1 - 14
Minimum guaranteed damage is set to 30%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter

invuln.strike: Invincible to strikes and projectiles from frames 1 - 14

messatsu-gohado.notes_sc: 5-hit Super projectile; projectile speed is not affected by button strength; can be used while OD projectiles are on-screen; slower startup allows opponents (except Dhalsim/Lily/Zangief) to avoid with a jump after the screen freeze

tenma-gozanku.name: Tenma Gozanku
notes: Fire a powerful ki blast from the air to the ground below. Useful for finishing combos from jump attacks that can be canceled.
During a neutral or forward jump
Minimum guaranteed damage is 30%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
tenma-gozanku.notes_sc: 5-hit Super projectile; during Forward or Neutral Jump only; 10f extra landing recovery on block/whiff; KD advantage varies significantly depending on angle and distance from opponent; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; travels down at ~45 degree angle; projectile speed/angle is not affected by button strength; projectile hitbox is considered an airborne attack (can be avoided with anti-air invuln moves); dissipates if Akuma is hit; can be used while OD projectiles are on-screen; leg hurtbox is projectile invuln until landing; crouching state on final recovery frame

empyreans-end.name: Empyrean’s End
notes: Turns the Satsui no Hado into a powerful flame before piercing the opponent. Opponents are open to follow-up attacks if they reach the end of the stage after being launched.
Completely invincible from frame 1 to frame 11.
Produces a wall splat if the opponent reaches the wall
Minimum guaranteed damage is set to 40%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter

invuln.full: Completely invincible from frame 1 to frame 11.

empyreans-end.notes_sc: Sends the opponent tumbling ~1/3 screen; causes wallsplat near corner (unless 214PP~6P wallsplat occurred earlier in the combo); the cinematic resets the opponent's juggle counter, allowing a follow-up wallsplat juggle regardless of previous juggle state; wallsplat is similar to Drive Impact, allowing moves to juggle that normally would not be possible; close-range opponent cannot jump away after the Super freeze to avoid chip damage

sip-of-calamity.name: Sip of Calamity
notes: Knocks down an opponent with overwhelming strength before unleashing a single, devastating blow. It can be canceled into from a special move, making it particularly useful in combos.
When under 25% vitality
Completely invincible from frame 1 to frame 11.
Minimum guaranteed damage is set to 50%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
* Can only be canceled into from special moves

invuln.full: Completely invincible from frame 1 to frame 11.

sip-of-calamity.notes_sc: Cinematic time regenerates ~1.9 Drive bars for Akuma; close-range opponent cannot jump away after the Super freeze to avoid chip damage; only 2f startup when canceled from 623P/214P/214P~6P; leaves opponent in range for microwalk 6HP pressure or Drive Rush oki

sip-of-calamity-critical-art.name: Sip of Calamity (Critical Art)
notes: Knocks down an opponent with overwhelming strength before unleashing a single, devastating blow. It can be canceled into from a special move, making it particularly useful in combos.
Completely invincible from frame 1 to frame 11.
Minimum guaranteed damage is set to 50%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
* Can only be canceled into from special moves

invuln.full: Completely invincible from frame 1 to frame 11.

shun-goku-satsu.name: Shun Goku Satsu
notes: A secret technique that overwhelms an opponent with myriad of attacks made in a single second. Cannot be avoided if performed at close range while the opponent is standing on the ground.
Completely invincible on frame 1
Invincible to projectiles from frames 6 - 27
Minimum guaranteed damage is set to 50%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
* Can only be canceled into from special moves

goshoha.name: Goshoha
notes: Grab your opponent, and send them flying away with two consecutive palm strikes. Throws your opponent without switching places with them.
When near opponent
Properties change to the following when the attack lands as a Punish Counter:
- Deals 2040 damage
- Super Art gauge increases by 4000
- Forces a hard knockdown
goshoha.notes_sc: Can walk or Dash + walk for manually-timed corner throw loop; immediate TK OD Air Fireball can stuff some wakeup reversals and leave Akuma plus on block; midscreen, cannot get strike/throw mixup but can Drive Rush into delayed 6HP to beat wakeup 4f normals (+1 oB); a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

shurettou.name: Shurettou
notes: Grab your opponent, and deliver a devastating downward chop. Throws your opponent while switching places with them.
When near opponent
Properties change to the following when the attack lands as a Punish Counter:
- Deals 2040 damage
- Super Art gauge increases by 4000
- Forces a hard knockdown
